entry.title: Termination processes and when Offboarding can be triggered
entry.content:
When an employee is terminated, all the necessary information to terminate the contract will be sent to the payroll system, and any other stakeholders will be informed. Standard offboarding will be triggered immediately once the termination is approved in Employee Central. But what happens if the person who resigned is a talent? Typically, organizations try to retain talent, and if the negotiation is successful, HR needs to proceed with the manual cancellation of the termination event and offboarding. It's also necessary to check if the termination has already been replicated in the payroll system and to remove the termination of the contract. In this blog, written with @BarbaraSerio ,there is an alternative solution to use offboarding as a pre-termination process.

Step to configure

  • Create an event reason 'Pre-termination' as a data change event. This way, the employee status will remain active.

  • If an event derivation rule is configured in Employee Central, add the event reason to the rule based on customer requirements (for example, based on the value of a specific field in the Job Information portlet).

  • Configure the business rule for Offboarding adding this event reason 

  • Configure a specific program for the negotiation 

HR can start the process by adding the data change event and offboarding will be triggered. If the negotiation fails, then a termination can be added manually in Employee Central.

entry.title: The top 5 HR trends today – and HR's guide to what's next
entry.content:
Each year, the HR Research Scientists at SAP SuccessFactors conduct research to understand the top HR and workforce trends facing organizations and share our perspective on what HR teams should consider as they look to help their companies address these trends. This year we aggregated and synthesized data from 40 global and regional reputable business press sources that put forward 254 individual trends and predictions grounded in their own research and data. We then conducted a content analysis of the trends sample to derive the five key themes, or “meta-trends.” While our annual report always includes some pointed commentary and critique about each trend based on our expertise in psychology, new this year is calling upon our own body of original applied research to incorporate datapoints and insights, resulting in a more evidence-based point of view. 

This year’s trends are in different stages of maturity and on different trajectories; therefore, the role that HR needs to play to help businesses tackle and capitalize on these trends is different. We’ve organized the trends into two sections aligned to the dual role HR will play in addressing them.  

First, HR will need to act as a Conductor, leading the orchestration of a strategy and associated change management across the business to realize the opportunities these trends offer:  

Trend #1: Reconnecting the disconnected employee: Contentious decisions, macroeconomic and sociopolitical stressors, and breached trust with leadership has led to employee stress and burnout – and consequently, a crisis of disconnect and counterproductivity. In the year ahead: 

  • Leaders must ruthlessly prioritize fulfilling their end of the “psychological contract” by meeting employees’ basic needs. 
  • People managers will be seen as a lifeline for employees drowning in disconnect. 
  • STAT: 57% of employees feel unless their companies make some serious changes, their burnout will not get better. 

Trend #2: Moving from AI hype to AI impact:Organizations are shifting from AI pilot projects to enterprise-wide rollouts, demanding proof of clear value and ROI. In the year ahead: 

  • Organizations will home in on their key value drivers for AI, revealing their true priorities. 
  • The body of research on the ROI of AI will be built this year. 
  • Organizations will find friction between leaders’ and employees’ goals for using AI. 
  • STAT: 46% of employees feel that the time that they save by using AI tools at work belongs to them, not their organization.​ 

Trend #3: Striking a balance to steer skills forward: Organizations continue to face pervasive skills gaps, in part due to rapid AI advancements. A more balanced approach is needed to see tangible progress in skills-based transformations this year. In the year ahead: 

  • “Skills-based” will no longer be the only goal.  
  • Pay will prove itself the missing piece of the upskilling puzzle.  
  • The human vs. technical skill debate will move from or to and. 
  • STAT: 54% of employees would be more motivated to learn new skills if their company instituted skills-based pay.​ 

Second, HR will need to act as a Navigator, leading the organization through precarious waters and circumventing obstacles to put policies into practice for the betterment of all stakeholders:  

Trend #4: Divesting or doubling down on diversity, equity, inclusions, and belonging (DEI&B): Some organizations remain committed to DEI&B goals, continuing to ask “How are we going to do this?” Others plan to divest, instead now asking “Are we going to do this?” In the year ahead: 

  • Some will shy away from DEI&B goals, but these approaches will vary.   
  • Taking a stand on DEI&B will change company cultures in the long term, but it’s not clear exactly how. 
  • STAT: 26% of employees say companies focus too much on DEI&B, 41% of employees say companies focus an appropriate amount on DEI&B, and 33% of employees say companies focus too little on DEI&B. 

Trend #5: Plugging into or pulling the plug on hybrid work: Now that organizations have determined their position on where their employees will work, it’s time to see if they achieve the outcomes they intended. In the year ahead: 

  • Those businesses choosing the return-to-office path will see whether their bets paid off this year. 
  • Those choosing the hybrid or remote path will take it a step further, integrating autonomy as a core value in other aspects of work design. 
  • STAT: 54% of employees would consider being paid less if they could have more flexibility in where and when they work.  

Read the report to see what’s now and what’s next for each trend, along with some fast facts that uplevel the nerdiness of this year’s trends report. We also include a section on how SAP SuccessFactors solutions can help organizations address the 2025 HR trends.

entry.title: Best Practices for Onboarding and MiniEC – With a Focus on Offboarding and Crossboarding
entry.content:
In this blog, the product management @emily_nagengast @Jayananda_Kotri  @smitajalit , product advisor @Shelly_Allen , partner management @PaulT , and knowledge expert team (I, @BarbaraSerio, @emicoke, @CarolinaCLeite, @Kanika_Khullar) would like to

share some insights about SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding with EC Mini Master and SAP On-premise as an external HRIS system.

What is Employee Central Mini-Master? 

Since Onboarding is built on the same platform as Employee Central it uses the same structure and data model. To maximize the benefits of Onboarding, it’s ideal for Employee Central to be already in place or implemented simultaneously. However in cases where the customer has not licensed Employee Central, a limited version of Employee Central - called EC Mini-Master needs to be used. EC Mini-Master provides the necessary structure and data model, but does not provide the same level of features as full Employee Central. Additionally, it’s important to understand that not all Onboarding features will be available. There are also configuration and setup considerations to keep in mind.

EC Mini-Master comes at no additional cost to the customer. When using EC Mini-Master its necessary to also differentiate between Full EC licensed users and a Functional EC licensed user. 

Typically a full EC licensed user would be an employee of the company, while a functional users would be external and not employed by the company. There are some other scenarios, and a full description can be found on the following help portal guide. Managing User License Compliance  

Below are the two user type scenarios and we will examine both in detail.

  • EC Mini-Master with functional users
  • EC Mini-Master with full EC licenses but full EC not yet implemented

EC Mini-Master with Functional Users

In this scenario, it's not possible to change employee data because no full licenses are available for Employee Central. For that reason, Crossboarding and Offboarding cannot be fully covered. However, there are no technical constraints to triggering those flows. The rules used in managing business configuration will be triggered even if the termination event, for example, is added from SAP on-prem with integration.
Please review the IDP (Implementation Design Principle) document below on custom integration.

SuccessFactors Integrations: Best Practices using SAP SuccessFactors APIs for Custom Integrations

If the employee, manager, and HR team focus solely on the tasks outlined in the programs without triggering any data review actions, the offboarding and crossboarding processes may be configured with limited functionality. For example, the manager can complete the welcome message, buddy assignment, recommended links for their new employee to see. However, the employee will not have any data entry tasks assigned during this process.

Note: Restarting onboarding is not supported when using EC Mini-Master with functional users.

Configuration steps/actions:

  1. SAP ERP HCM mapping configuration for replication of employee data on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Mini Master. Please refer to Employee Data Replication in this blog.
  2. Configuration of business rule on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Center Mini Master to trigger Offboarding.
  3. Terminate an employee in SAP ERP HCM.

4. Run replication report on SAP ERP HCM. After replication, the termination is updated in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Mini Master.

5. Intelligent Service is triggered and offboardee lands in the dashboard.        

 

Employee Data Replication

Employee Data replication, as per Best Practice, must be implemented using Business Integration Builder (BIB). BIB framework includes configuration layer, reports for triggering Organizational and Employees Data replication on backend side, it takes advantage of prepackaged Integration Flows on BTP Integration Suite and of the SuccessFactors oData API to upsert data into Employee Central entities (that means Crossboarding and Offboarding processes can be triggered from every external tool using SuccessFactors oData APIs for updating employees data).

BIB configuration documentation for replicating Employee and Organizational Data from SAP ERP HCM to Employee Central can be found at following link: Introduction to Employee Data Replication to Employee Central

 

EC Mini-Master with Full EC Licenses, Pending Full EC Implementation

In this scenario, employee data can potentially be changed. Therefore, full offboarding and crossboarding processes can be covered. Below are some do's and don'ts for this scenario.

Leading practice recommends having one system as the source of changes for employee data. We recommend leveraging employee events in SAP ERP HCM and then updating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Center Mini Master.

We don't recommend using hybrid scenarios (some events generated in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Center Mini Master and others in SAP ERP HCM) just because the customer has full licenses of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Center. As a leading practice, a full implementation of Employee Central (EC) is recommended. This hybrid scenario can generate inconsistencies and require many custom integrations. However, it is possible to start the employee data review by asking, for example, the offboardee to change their email to a personal one. It is crucial, however, to ensure that the updated information is also reflected in SAP ERP HCM to avoid inconsistencies or data discrepancies. 

In order to configure the solution mentioned in this blog, you will need to engage with SAP Professional Services or Certified Implementation Partner.

In addition to the information shared in the blog above, we would encourage our partners to view the following document which outlines for configuration partners some of the additional considerations when using EC Mini-Master and how this impacts both Onboarding and additional processes. 
A partner S-user is required to access this document - Onboarding with EC Mini Master Scoping and Considerations

Summary

Ideally, we recommend that all customers have full implementation of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, as it offers the best user experience—not just during Onboarding but as part of a comprehensive HR system. However, we hope the information above is helpful if you encounter customer scenarios where this isn’t the case. If you have any further questions, please post them below and tag the authors.

entry.title: Crossboarding process (internal hire process) should trigger from Recruitment Management Only
entry.content:
Crossboarding process (internal hire process) should trigger from Recruitment Management and restrict to trigger from employee central

Introduction

We would like to share our experiences of using Crossboarding process (Internal Hire Process) should trigger for internal employees only from Recruitment Management meanwhile should restrict to trigger from Employee central. It provides solution to complex client requirements for trigger crossboarding process.

In general, as per the standard functional in SuccessFactors, Crossboarding process will trigger from Recruitment Management and as well as from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

Here, we are presenting you how to trigger Crossboarding process from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 only and meanwhile will show how to restrict to trigger Crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0. This blog will help you address similar requirements in your projects.

Trends/Problem Statement / Overview

Scenario 1: The Crossboarding process should trigger from Recruitment Management to  Onboarding 2.0.

Scenario 2: The Crossboarding process should restrict to trigger from Employee central to Onboarding 2.0.

Challenges

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, the primary challenge is that we are unable to control the restriction of internal hire records in the Onboarding dashboard even when transfer events are restricted from business rules, the Onboarding dashboard will still display empty transfer event records when transferring from Employee Central as shown below.

Solution

The following steps will trigger Crossboarding process from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and as well as restrict to trigger from Employee central to Onboarding 2.0.

  • We can clone an existing process variant and modify the existing process flow by adding process steps to suit customer requirement.
  • Create custom Field “Source System for Onboarding” in Job Information portlet.

  • The following business rule pick the Crossboarding process variant after initiate the onboarding process from RCM to Onboarding 2.0

  • The following business rule restrict to trigger crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

  • The following business trigger Crossboarding process based on the event reasons derived in the business rule.

  • The following business rules need to be place under Job Information in Manage business configuration. Go to Manage Business Configuration, under HRIS Elements select jobInfo. Scroll down to the trigger rules section and set the Transfer rule for the Event Type as onPostSave.

Expected Output / Outcome

Scenario 1:  As soon as initiated Crossboarding from RCM, The Crossboarding process initiated successfully and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard.

Scenario 2: As soon as initiated Crossboarding from Employee Central, The Crossboarding process will not trigger, and internal hire record will not appear in Onboarding dashboard.

2.Benefits

It provides solution to complex client requirements to configure crossboarding process. It will help to restrict to trigger Crossboarding from Employee Central.

It provides solutions to trigger specific transfer events can trigger from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and can trigger specific transfer events within employee central module (for example trigger promotion event reasons from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and demotion event reasons trigger only in Employee central).

The main benefits are as follows:

  • Hiring Manager / Responsible person can review the only specific event reasons internal hire data during Onboarding process.
  • By restricting the trigger of specific event reasons in the internal hire process from Employee Central, manual intervention of the Hiring Manager/Responsible Persons' efforts during the Onboarding process can be reduced.

3.Conclusion

In general, as per the standard functional in SuccessFactors, Crossboarding process will trigger from Recruitment Management and as well as from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0. This document will help to the customers who don’t need the Crossboarding process to trigger from Employee central. The detailed configuration steps of this document will helpful how to trigger Crossboarding process from RCM to Onboarding 2.0 only and meanwhile will show how to restrict to trigger Crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

The customizable nature of SAP SuccessFactors allows organizations to tailor the Crossboarding processes to their specific requirements.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: National ID integration in between Recruitment Management and Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
Introduction

We would like to share our experiences of using National ID mapping Integration in between Recruitment Management and Onboarding 2.0. It provides solution to complex client requirements to integrated candidate national ID information only for selected countries in between RCM and ONB 2.0. Here, we are presenting you how to integrate national ID information from RCM to Onboarding 2.0 for only selected countries. This will help you address similar requirements in your projects.

In general, National ID information will capture by New Hire in personal data collection step during Onboarding 2.0 process. But in our current project, the client has running operations in 80+ countries, only USA external candidate should capture National ID information in RCM and rest of the country’s external candidates will capture during Personal Data Collection step in Onboarding process.

Trends/Problem Statement / Overview

Scenario 1:  The customer wants to capture new hire national ID information for USA only in Recruitment management and it should be integrated with Onboarding 2.0

Scenario 2: Rest of the countries national ID will capture during Onboarding process (Rest of the countries, new hires will capture national ID during Onboarding process)

Challenges

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, if we do national ID mapping in the recruit to hire data field mapping in Onboarding, the system will have limitations because the national ID mapping will apply to all countries after initiating onboarding from Recruitment and the result is as follows:

  • The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment.
  • The onboarding process will fail for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment and candidates will appear in Onboarding BPE error tile.

Solution

Scenario 1 (For example USA):

  • We need to create specific job application template for USA and there we need to define the field for SSN. So, external candidate will capture SSN during the recruitment process.
  • Configured Business Rule as shown in below

 

  • Placed business rule in Manage Rules in Recruitment as shown in below

 

  • National ID mapping in recruit to hire data field mapping.

  • Create an SAP incident in SAP launchpad and request SAP to run the script

Please find below the KBA for more information.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3048929

 

Scenario 2 (Global Countries) :

  • We need to create specific application template for global countries without national ID field. So, external candidate will capture national ID information during the Onboarding process.

Expected Output / Outcome

Scenario 1:  As soon as initiated Onboarding from RCM, the onboarding process initiated successfully for USA and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard. The responsible group user (who will do review new hire data step) can see SSN information in review new hire data step.

Scenario 2: As soon as initiated Onboarding from RCM, the onboarding process initiated successfully for Global countries and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard.

Benefits

The main benefits are as follows:

  • It provides solutions to trigger Onboarding process for only specific countries from Recruitment where The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment.
  • The rehire process for candidates who have national ID details captured during the recruitment process will be determined by the system and they will be placed in the first rehire check by matching their exact profile.
  • The onboarding process will get success for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment.

Conclusion

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, if we do national ID mapping in the recruit to hire data field mapping in Onboarding, the system will have limitations because the national ID mapping will apply to all countries. This document will help to the customers who need the National ID information for specific countries during the recruitment process. The detailed configuration steps of this document will helpful to trigger Onboarding process for only specific countries from Recruitment where The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment and simultaneously the onboarding process will get success for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: Webinar: Beyond Integration - How PepsiCo Builds Custom HR Applications with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Many organizations have a need to build custom HR applications and extensions to complement their SAP SuccessFactors solutions. As part of Comentec’s “SAP BTP for HR” webinar series, this episode moves beyond integration to explore the SAP Business Technology Platform's full potential for HR solutions.

Please join SAP BTP and SAP SuccessFactors partner, Comentec and PepsiCo's HR Technology team to demonstrate how SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables HR and HRIS to efficiently create high-quality custom HR extensions with limited development effort.

Using a Talent Card application as an example, Comentec will showcase how BTP's built-in templates, standards, and security features are combined to create robust solutions that HR technology teams can easily maintain while PepsiCo will share their firsthand experience of using BTP to develop a secure, sustainable HR application that utilizes data from SAP SuccessFactors.

When:  Date: April 2, 2025. |  Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link

Join us to discover how SAP BTP's standardized components and best practices can help you:

  • Develop custom applications for SAP SuccessFactors with reduced effort and complexity
  • Create solutions that are easier to maintain and support internally
  • Leverage SAP's standard technology stack for improved sustainability
  • Build a stronger business case for custom application development

This session will help you reimagine the possibilities of BTP and provide you with the confidence to evaluate how it can be leveraged across your project roadmap.

This webinar is designed for:

  • Strategic decision-makers evaluating Employee Central and extensibility options
  • Technical leaders managing existing HR environment seeking modernization strategies
  • Business stakeholders wanting to improve data access, user experience, and HR processes.

Event info:
Date: April 2, 2025.
Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link- this will take you to the registration page hosted by Comentec.

SAP Build Apps SAP Build Code SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Configuring Reimbursement Eligibility Cycles in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizations often impose restrictions on the frequency of reimbursement claims for specific benefits. A common requirement is to allow certain reimbursements—such as Mobile Handset Reimbursement—only once every three years.

While SAP SuccessFactors Benefits Administration provides standard Frequency and Maximum Claims Per Frequency settings within Schedule and Claim Dates, there is no predefined option for a three-year frequency. However, this requirement can be met by leveraging the Annualization Factor.

To understand how this works, let’s first explore the Annualization Factor and its role in SAP SuccessFactors.

Understanding the Annualization Factor (Step 1)

What is the Annualization Factor?

The Annualization Factor in SAP SuccessFactors is a key parameter used to determine the frequency of an event over a standard annual cycle. It defines how often a benefit or entitlement should be available by expressing it as a fraction of a year.

For example:

  • Annual Frequency (default) has an Annualization Factor of 1, meaning the benefit is available once per year.
  • Quarterly Frequency has an Annualization Factor of 4, making the benefit available four times a year (every 3 months).
  • A Two-Year Frequency would have an Annualization Factor of 0.5, meaning the benefit becomes available once every two years.

This factor translates time-based frequencies into a standardized annual model, allowing the system to control claim eligibility accordingly.

Applying the Annualization Factor in Reimbursements

Within the Benefits Administration module, the Annualization Factor is particularly useful for setting up custom reimbursement cycles that are not available in standard configurations.

For instance, if a Mobile Handset Reimbursement should only be available once every three years, setting an Annualization Factor of 0.333 (1/3) ensures that:

  • The system restricts any new claims until the three-year period has passed.
  • Employees can only submit a claim again after three years from their last approved claim.

Now that we have established the importance of the Annualization Factor, let’s move on to configuring a custom frequency in SAP SuccessFactors.

Creating a Custom Frequency (Step 2)

Since SAP SuccessFactors does not provide a predefined three-year frequency, we need to create one before assigning it to the reimbursement settings.

Steps to Create a Custom Frequency:

  1. Navigate to Manage Organization, Pay, and Job Structures.
  2. Select the Frequency object.
  3. Create a new frequency with the following details:
    • Frequency Name: Three-Year Frequency
    • Code: THREE_YEARS (or any relevant identifier as per naming conventions)
    • Annualization Factor: 0.333 (Ensures eligibility resets once every three years.)
  4. Save the newly created frequency.

Once this custom frequency is created, it becomes available for selection in the Benefits configuration.

Configuring the Reimbursement Schedule (Step 3)

Now that the Three-Year Frequency has been created, it needs to be assigned to the reimbursement type to enforce the required eligibility cycle.

Steps to Assign the Custom Frequency in the Reimbursement Schedule:

  1. Navigate to Benefits Administration module.
  2. Open the Reimbursement Schedule settings for the relevant reimbursement type.
  3. Assign the Frequency:
    • Select Three-Year Frequency (created in Step 2) from the available options.
  4. Define Claim Limits:
    • Set "Maximum Claims Per Frequency" to 1 (Ensures employees can apply only once per three-year period.)
  5. Save the configuration.

Expected System Behavior (Step 4)

To illustrate how the system enforces this restriction, consider the following scenario:

  • An employee submits a reimbursement claim on 17/03/2025.
  • Due to the applied configuration:
    • The employee cannot apply again before 18/03/2028.
    • Any attempt to submit a claim before 18/03/2028 will be restricted by the system.
    • A new claim can only be submitted after 17/03/2028.

Validating the Configuration (Step 5)

To ensure the setup is working correctly, perform the following test cases:

Test Case Action Expected Outcome
Test Case 1Employee submits a claim on 17/03/2025✅Successfully processed
Test Case 2Employee attempts to submit another claim on 16/03/2028❌System restricts the visibility of the claim
Test Case 3Employee reapplies on 18/03/2028✅Reimbursement Eligible

Conclusion

By leveraging a Custom Frequency with an Annualization Factor of 0.333, SAP SuccessFactors can effectively enforce reimbursement eligibility cycles spanning multiple years—without requiring any custom development.

This structured approach ensures:
✅Policy Compliance – Enforces multi-year eligibility cycles seamlessly.
✅Reduced Manual Intervention – Automates claim restrictions.
✅Enhanced Employee Experience – Employees clearly understand their eligibility window.

💡Have you encountered similar scenarios in your SAP SuccessFactors implementation? Share your experiences and insights in the comments below! 🚀

entry.title: EC Diagnostic Tool :SAP Successfactors Onboarding
entry.content:
This Blog was built together with @SanchayitaKundu and @Angela_Romano  .

As you aware Successfactors EC Team has introduced Diagnostic tool analysis for Succesfactors Module in 1H2024 Release. In this blog , we will cover how Diagnostic Blog can be utilized in Onboarding Module.

​This tool enables self-diagnosis and self-analysis of the complete data transaction and workflow processing in a single view, allowing customers or internal users to resolve issues independently and reduce resolution time.

​Supported Transaction Types:

  • Workflows
  • Centralized Services Save Actions
  • Snapshot View
  • Rule Execution View

​RBP to enable tool:

  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Diagnostics Tracing Configuration
  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Workflows Diagnostics Data
  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Centralized Services Diagnostics Data
  • Administrator > Admin Center Permissions > Access Diagnostic Tool 

​

​Note:
We recommend creating a permission group to restrict access to this tool to only a few people/admins. This tool does not respect RBP access at field levels therefore the provided admins can see restricted employee's data. To avoid such issues or concerns please create a separate permission role for this access and give the access to few admins/users so that it can be easily monitored and controlled.

Save Action Transaction:

  • Snapshot View shows the snapshots or the records starting from the fields that were changed till the related changes, rules, end date corrections, forward propagation and final save action. In each stage a tabular data will be shown for all related cross-entities or sub-entities involved. We can compare the field changes in each stage to get an idea of which stage the field values changed.
  • Rule Execution view shows in detail all the records that were filtered out for rule execution with reason, all the rules that were filtered out with reason and all the rules that got executed with timestamp and details of the fields and it's values the rule changed. It gives functionality to search by rule ID or field ID for cases where several rules got executed.
  • After enabling the tool, the issue needs to be replicated and then the transaction trace is available for issue analysis.
  • Tracing will turn off after 15 minutes. The traced data will auto delete after 10 days.
  • The tool doesn't support MDF objects for now.
  • Only onSave rules data are captured.
  • Mass changes, API and imports save transactions are not traced.

Save Action Configuration: Business Action should be Onboarding.

  • In Manage Data, set up the Diagnostics Tracing Configuration MDF object.  You may also use the direct link in diagnostic tool page, where a link to "Enable Tracing" is provided.
  • In Diagnostic Trace Configuration, create new or modify existing trace, by entering any code, status= active and Activate Diagnostic Trace = Yes.
  • Enter user name of user completing the transaction in Requested By field (if using proxy enter that user's name). 
  • Requested For field doesn’t support external onboarding users yet

  • Save the object and then replicate the save action on a user.

  • Once this object is saved from the start time provided the tracing will be active for 15 minutes.

  • Within this time frame, any Save transaction executed by the requested user would be captured and available in the tracing details.

  • Business Action enlists all supported ChangeSources.

    • New Hire Data Review / Access New Hire Data / Personal Data Collection/Final Data Review /Hire Template with standard HRIS Element- Onboarding
    • Manage Pending Hires – Hire/Rehire Processes
    • Offboarding / RHDR Pages /MDF pages is not supported

    • For PDC page :

         A) if manager completed PDC on behalf of onboardee ,then we capture this transaction ? - Yes, since the 'Requested By' is an internal employee and searchable on the tool configuration.   
    •    B)If PDC completed by onboardee ,do we support transaction ? - Currently this information is not captured since 'Requested By' doesn't allow search of External Onboarding Users

Workflow Tracing:

  • The approval or actions on the workflow should be done only from "workflow details page".
  • If the workflows are approved from the "home page quick action" or "mobile device" or "Manage workflow requests" admin tool by taking action on the workflows by admin and changing workflow approvers or status, then such changes will not be traced. In the diagnostic tool for such scenarios only the initiate data with pending workflow status will be shown because other approval or send back actions was not traced.
  • Workflows tracing will start and keep capturing from the date the Role-based permission (RBP) is provided to the Diagnostic tool > Workflow Configuration Data. However, after 10 days of no action on the workflow the tracing data will get auto-deleted. If any action is done on the workflow like approval or send back, the tracing data will stay for + 10 days and so on.
  • Auto-approval or CC role workflows are supported.
  • Workflows based on HRIS Elements are only supported.
  • Workflow trace will be only if the workflow is actually submitted. No simulation tracing is available.

Workflow Tracing Transaction

Search fields for Workflow Tracing:

  • Requested By = user doing the transaction
  • Requested For = user whose data is getting changed in the transaction
  • Date Range = Workflow initiate date Or when the changes on employee was done/ issue replication date. At a time only 3 days can be selected and searched.

Thanks,

Gopinath M
